_comment: DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B-it-NVFP4 on an L4 (vLLM) WITH live web search for deep research. vLLM native tool calling via the gemma4 parser: --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser gemma4 --reasoning-parser gemma4 (per the official recipe https://recipes.vllm.ai/Google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it — built-in chat_template handles tools, so NO --chat-template override; thinking STAYS ON, routed to reasoning_content by --reasoning-parser gemma4). openclaw.compat.supportsTools=true gates native tool_calls. compat.contextWindow + --max-model-len raised to 32768 so the agent+tools+web prompt fits (the plain 4096 overflowed even on 'what is my name?'; KV ~0.7 GiB at 32768, well within the L4). web/identity reuse the validated T4 wiring (Brave plugin install, USER.md identity); BRAVE_API_KEY forwarded from ~/.env by the launcher. VERIFIED on L4 2026-06-18: served; gemma4 emitted native tool_calls under block-diffusion decode; web_search executed REAL Brave searches (Python 3.14 + cited python.org URL); 'Your name is Hiroki'; finishReason stop, no tag leakage. Known multi-step edge: a 3rd accumulated tool-using step can hit OpenClaw's auto-compaction bug ('Already compacted') — single-search research is clean; for heavier multi-step raise --max-model-len/contextWindow further (e.g. 65536, proven on the T4/Ollama path; KV still fits the L4). FALLBACKS if tool_calls ever come back empty/garbled: (a) add --chat-template <tool_chat_template_gemma4.jinja>, or (b) codeMode (compat.supportsTools=false + web.code_mode=true, drop --tool-call-parser).

vllm.install_command: python -m pip install -U vllm --pre --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/nightly/cu129 --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu129
